ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 (Wicked Panda) public-facing-app exploitation → web-server-spawned LOLBin download chain
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical APT41 / BARIUM intrusion opener: exploitation of a public-facing app (CVE-2021-40539 ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus, CVE-2019-19781 Citrix, CVE-2020-10189 Zoho, CVE-2022-29464 WSO2) where the compromised web/Java process directly spawns cmd / PowerShell / certutil / bitsadmin to fetch second-stage payloads (HIGHNOON, CROSSWALK, ShadowPad). FireEye/Mandiant attributed the 2021-2022 mass-exploitation campaign hitting >75 organisations across 20 countries to this exact chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe","php-cgi.exe","ws-jvm.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe") AND (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*-urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*-decode*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*WebClient*" OR Processes.process="*Transfer *" OR Processes.process="*/AddOnUpdates/*" OR Processes.process="*ServiceDesk*") by host, user, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.parent_process, Processes.process_name, Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_name as ParentProcess, parent_process as ParentCmd, process_name as ChildProcess, process as ChildCmd | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WebSrvParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe","php-cgi.exe","ws-jvm.exe"]);
let LolBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebSrvParents)
| where FileName in~ (LolBins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-urlcache","-decode","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","WebClient","Transfer ","/AddOnUpdates/","ServiceDesk")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentFolder  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ChildProcess  = FileName,
          ChildCmd      = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSha256   = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WebSrvParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe","php-cgi.exe","ws-jvm.exe"]);
let LolBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (WebSrvParents)
| where ChildLeaf in (LolBins)
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-urlcache","-decode","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","WebClient","Transfer ","/AddOnUpdates/","ServiceDesk")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcess = ParentProcessName,
          ChildProcess  = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Web-Server Process Spawns LOLBin Downloader
id: 7c2d18a1-a41a-4cf2-9f8d-apt41-webserver-lolbin
status: experimental
description: Detects APT41 / Wicked Panda public-facing-app exploitation chain — IIS/Tomcat/Java/Nginx process directly spawns cmd, PowerShell, certutil, or bitsadmin with download / decode flags.
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat8.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\node.exe'
      - '\php-cgi.exe'
      - '\ws-jvm.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  selection_cmdline:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - '-urlcache'
      - '-decode'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - 'WebClient'
      - 'Transfer '
      - '/AddOnUpdates/'
      - 'ServiceDesk'
  condition: all of selection_*
fields:
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - ComputerName
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate web app deployment / patching scripts
  - In-house service-desk auto-updaters
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.g0096
ucs.rationale: APT41 is the cluster Mandiant attributed to the 2021–2022 ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus / Zoho / WSO2 mass-exploitation campaigns. Their post-exploit pattern is unusual among China-nexus actors: instead of dropping a second binary the threat actor immediately runs `cmd /c certutil -urlcache -split -f http://… payload` or `powershell -nop iex (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString(...)` direct
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 (Winnti / Brass Typhoon) DLL search-order side-loading via signed-binary-in-user-path (ShadowPad / PlugX / CROSSWALK loader)
ucs.description: Hunts APT41's signature loader pattern: a legitimately-signed third-party or Microsoft binary copied into a user-writable directory (Public, ProgramData, Temp, AppData) where it side-loads an adjacent unsigned/non-Microsoft DLL — the established delivery mechanism for ShadowPad, PlugX variants, and the CROSSWALK / POISONPLUG.SHADOW / Winnti loaders that Mandiant, Kaspersky, and ESET have repeatedly tied to this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*") AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Windows\\*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Program Files*" AND (Processes.signature="Microsoft*" OR Processes.signature="Google*" OR Processes.signature="VMware*" OR Processes.signature="Symantec*" OR Processes.signature="Kaspersky*" OR Processes.signature="Trend Micro*" OR Processes.signature="McAfee*" OR Processes.signature="Bitdefender*" OR Processes.signature="Citrix*" OR Processes.signature="Adobe*" OR Processes.signature="Hewlett*" OR Processes.signature="NVIDIA*" OR Processes.signature="Intel*") AND Processes.process_name!="msiexec.exe" by host, user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process_path, Processes.process, Processes.signature, Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_path [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Endpoint.Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by host, Endpoint.Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name("Endpoint.Filesystem")` | rex field=file_path "^(?<process_path>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+\.dll$"] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let KnownVendors = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Google LLC","Google Inc","VMware, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","Trend Micro Inc.","McAfee, Inc.","Bitdefender SRL","Citrix Systems, Inc.","Adobe Systems Incorporated","Adobe Inc.","Hewlett-Packard","Hewlett Packard Enterprise","NVIDIA Corporation","Intel Corporation","Cisco Systems, Inc."]);
let SuspiciousLaunch =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName in~ (KnownVendors)
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
    | where FileName !~ "msiexec.exe"
    | project LaunchTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SignedExe=FileName, SignedFolder=FolderPath,
              SignedCompany=ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              SignedSha=SHA256, ProcessId;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| join kind=inner SuspiciousLaunch on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ProcessId
| where Timestamp between (LaunchTime .. LaunchTime + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          SignedExe, SignedCompany, SignedFolder, SignedSha,
          SideloadedDll = FileName, DllSha = SHA256, DllSize = FileSize
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let KnownVendors = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Google LLC","Google Inc","VMware, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","Trend Micro Inc.","McAfee, Inc.","Bitdefender SRL","Citrix Systems, Inc.","Adobe Systems Incorporated","Adobe Inc.","Hewlett-Packard","Hewlett Packard Enterprise","NVIDIA Corporation","Intel Corporation","Cisco Systems, Inc."]);
let SuspiciousLaunch =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ActingProcessFileCompany in~ (KnownVendors)
    | where TargetProcessName has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
    | where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files\"
    | where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
    | extend ExeFolder = strcat_array(array_slice(split(TargetProcessName,"\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
    | project LaunchTime=TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              SignedExe=TargetProcessName, ExeFolder,
              SignedCompany=ActingProcessFileCompany,
              SignedSha=SHA256, TargetProcessId;
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         LoadedHashes= tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         LoadedSigned= tostring(EventData.Signed),
         ProcGuid    = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| extend LoadedFolder = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded,"\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         ImageFolder  = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image,"\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where LoadedFolder =~ ImageFolder
| join kind=inner SuspiciousLaunch on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname, $left.Image == $right.SignedExe
| where TimeGenerated between (LaunchTime .. LaunchTime + 5m)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ActorUsername,
          SignedExe = Image, SignedCompany, SignedSha,
          SideloadedDll = ImageLoaded, LoadedHashes, LoadedSigned
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: DLL search-order side-loading from a renamed/relocated signed binary is the single most consistent technique across the APT41 / Winnti / Brass Typhoon malware estate — Mandiant's POISONPLUG.SHADOW / CROSSWALK loaders, Kaspersky's ShadowPad reports, and ESET's Winnti tracking all describe the same shape: a legitimate signed exe (often Microsoft, McAfee, Symantec, VMware, or Bitdefender utilities) i
